item.text:
`allow()` already has the requested pruning logic at [demo/rate_limiter.py](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-uglee-Coding-ralphton-research/16c236c5-e508-4436-9d28-e8588d7300ac/scratchpad/omb-grid-nohook/demo/rate_limiter.py:15):

```python
self.calls = [t for t in self.calls if now - t < self.window]
```

I verified behavior with a deterministic inline script: with `now = 112.0`, calls `[100.0, 105.0, 111.0]`, `limit=2`, `window=10`, it pruned `100.0` and left `[105.0, 111.0]`, then correctly returned `False`.

I didn’t apply an edit because the file already contains the requested rewrite. Git status/diff couldn’t complete in this read-only environment because Git’s macOS helper tried to create temp/cache files under `/tmp`.
